infobox.value: or **Rotorua District** is a [territorial authority](/wiki/Territorial_authorities_of_New_Zealand "Territorial authorities of New Zealand") district in the North Island of New Zealand. It has one urban area of significant size, the city of [Rotorua](/wiki/Rotorua "Rotorua"). The district is governed by Rotorua Lakes Council, which is headquartered in Rotorua and is headed by a mayor. The district falls within two [regional council](/wiki/Regions_of_New_Zealand "Regions of New Zealand") areas, with the majority of the area and Rotorua city in the [Bay of Plenty Region](/wiki/Bay_of_Plenty_Region "Bay of Plenty Region") and the rest in the [Waikato](/wiki/Waikato "Waikato") region.[[3]](#cite_note-3) [Tania Tapsell](/wiki/Tania_Tapsell "Tania Tapsell") has been the [mayor of Rotorua](/wiki/Mayor_of_Rotorua "Mayor of Rotorua") since the [2022 local elections](/wiki/2022_local_elections "2022 local elections").

For the lakes, see [Lakes of Rotorua](/wiki/Lakes_of_Rotorua "Lakes of Rotorua").

**Rotorua Lakes District** or **Rotorua District** is a [territorial authority](/wiki/Territorial_authorities_of_New_Zealand "Territorial authorities of New Zealand") district in the North Island of New Zealand. It has one urban area of significant size, the city of [Rotorua](/wiki/Rotorua "Rotorua"). The district is governed by Rotorua Lakes Council, which is headquartered in Rotorua and is headed by a mayor. The district falls within two [regional council](/wiki/Regions_of_New_Zealand "Regions of New Zealand") areas, with the majority of the area and Rotorua city in the [Bay of Plenty Region](/wiki/Bay_of_Plenty_Region "Bay of Plenty Region") and the rest in the [Waikato](/wiki/Waikato "Waikato") region.[[3]](#cite_note-3) [Tania Tapsell](/wiki/Tania_Tapsell "Tania Tapsell") has been the [mayor of Rotorua](/wiki/Mayor_of_Rotorua "Mayor of Rotorua") since the [2022 local elections](/wiki/2022_local_elections "2022 local elections").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_District&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Rotorua has an unusual history, as the town was built by the Government as a tourist destination in the 1880s.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Through the Rotorua Borough Act 1922, which achieved [royal assent](/wiki/Royal_assent "Royal assent") on 28 September 1922, the Rotorua Borough was formed.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The inaugural elections for mayor were held in February 1923 and [Cecil Clinkard](/wiki/Cecil_Clinkard "Cecil Clinkard") was successful.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 1962, Rotorua was proclaimed a city.[[8]](#cite_note-8) In 1979, the status was changed to a district when Rotorua City and Rotorua County amalgamated.[[9]](#cite_note-9) The district council held its first meeting on 2 April 1979.[[10]](#cite_note-10) At the 27 November 2014 council meeting, it was decided to change the operating name of the council to Rotorua Lakes Council, while the official name would remain unchanged.[[11]](#cite_note-11) Since then, the district has been known as Rotorua Lakes[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] and the council's web domain changed from *www.rdc.govt.nz* to *rotorualakescouncil.nz*.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_District&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Rotorua Lakes covers 2,409 square kilometres (930 sq mi).[[12]](#cite_note-12) The district's area is 61.52% in the Bay of Plenty region and 38.48% in the Waikato region. Adjacent districts (in a clockwise direction starting in the north) are [Western Bay of Plenty](/wiki/Western_Bay_of_Plenty_District "Western Bay of Plenty District"), [Whakatāne](/wiki/Whakat%C4%81ne_District "Whakatāne District"), [Taupō](/wiki/Taup%C5%8D_District "Taupō District"), and [South Waikato](/wiki/South_Waikato_District "South Waikato District").[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)

The Bay of Plenty portion of the district includes the settlements of [Rotorua](/wiki/Rotorua "Rotorua"), [Mamaku](/wiki/Mamaku "Mamaku"), [Hamurana](/wiki/Hamurana "Hamurana"), [Mourea](/wiki/Mourea "Mourea"), [Rotoiti Forest](/wiki/Rotoiti_Forest "Rotoiti Forest"), [Lake Rotoma](/w/index.php?title=Lake_Rotoma_(locality)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lake Rotoma (locality) (page does not exist)"), [Lake Okareka](/w/index.php?title=Lake_Okareka_(locality)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lake Okareka (locality) (page does not exist)"), [Lake Tarawera](/w/index.php?title=Lake_Tarawera_(locality)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Lake Tarawera (locality) (page does not exist)"), [Rerewhakaaitu](/w/index.php?title=Rerewhakaaitu&action=edit&redlink=1 "Rerewhakaaitu (page does not exist)"), and [Kaingaroa Forest](/wiki/Kaingaroa,_Bay_of_Plenty "Kaingaroa, Bay of Plenty"). The Waikato portion includes the settlements of [Waiotapu](/wiki/Waiotapu "Waiotapu"), [Reporoa](/wiki/Reporoa "Reporoa"), [Broadlands](/w/index.php?title=Broadlands,_New_Zealand&action=edit&redlink=1 "Broadlands, New Zealand (page does not exist)"), [Mihi](/wiki/Mihi,_New_Zealand "Mihi, New Zealand"), [Waikite Valley](/wiki/Waikite_Valley "Waikite Valley"), [Ngakuru](/wiki/Ngakuru "Ngakuru"), and [Ātiamuri](/wiki/%C4%80tiamuri "Ātiamuri").

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_District&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Demographics")]

Rotorua District covers 2,409.31 km2 (930.24 sq mi)[[1]](#cite_note-Area-1) and had an estimated population of 78,000 as of June 2025,[[2]](#cite_note-NZ_population_data_2018-2) with a population density of 32 people per km2.

Historical population

| Year | Pop. | ±% p.a. |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2006 | 65,901 | — |
| 2013 | 65,280 | −0.14% |
| 2018 | 71,877 | +1.94% |
| 2023 | 74,058 | +0.60% |
| Source: [[15]](#cite_note-Census_2018-15)[[16]](#cite_note-Census_2023-16) | | |

Rotorua District had a population of 74,058 in the [2023 New Zealand census](/wiki/2023_New_Zealand_census "2023 New Zealand census"), an increase of 2,181 people (3.0%) since the [2018 census](/wiki/2018_New_Zealand_census "2018 New Zealand census"), and an increase of 8,778 people (13.4%) since the [2013 census](/wiki/2013_New_Zealand_census "2013 New Zealand census"). There were 36,336 males, 37,491 females and 234 people of [other genders](/wiki/Non-binary_gender "Non-binary gender") in 25,905 dwellings.[[17]](#cite_note-17) 2.6% of people identified as [LGBTIQ+](/wiki/LGBTQ "LGBTQ"). The median age was 36.6 years (compared with 38.1 years nationally). There were 16,053 people (21.7%) aged under 15 years, 14,076 (19.0%) aged 15 to 29, 32,358 (43.7%) aged 30 to 64, and 11,571 (15.6%) aged 65 or older.[[16]](#cite_note-Census_2023-16)

People could identify as more than one ethnicity. The results were 61.6% [European](/wiki/European_New_Zealanders "European New Zealanders") ([Pākehā](/wiki/P%C4%81keh%C4%81 "Pākehā")); 43.5% [Māori](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_people "Māori people"); 6.4% [Pasifika](/wiki/Pasifika_New_Zealanders "Pasifika New Zealanders"); 10.1% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_New_Zealanders "Asian New Zealanders"); 0.7% Middle Eastern, Latin American and African New Zealanders (MELAA); and 2.0% other, which includes people giving their ethnicity as "New Zealander". English was spoken by 96.0%, Māori language by 13.6%, Samoan by 0.4% and other languages by 9.8%. No language could be spoken by 2.2% (e.g. too young to talk). [New Zealand Sign Language](/wiki/New_Zealand_Sign_Language "New Zealand Sign Language") was known by 0.6%. The percentage of people born overseas was 17.9, compared with 28.8% nationally.

Religious affiliations were 30.9% [Christian](/wiki/Christianity_in_New_Zealand "Christianity in New Zealand"), 1.7% [Hindu](/wiki/Hinduism_in_New_Zealand "Hinduism in New Zealand"), 0.3% [Islam](/wiki/Islam_in_New_Zealand "Islam in New Zealand"), 3.6% [Māori religious beliefs](/wiki/Religion_of_M%C4%81ori_people "Religion of Māori people"), 0.7% [Buddhist](/wiki/Buddhism_in_New_Zealand "Buddhism in New Zealand"), 0.4% [New Age](/wiki/New_Age "New Age"), 0.1% [Jewish](/wiki/Judaism_in_New_Zealand "Judaism in New Zealand"), and 1.8% other religions. People who answered that they had [no religion](/wiki/Irreligion_in_New_Zealand "Irreligion in New Zealand") were 53.3%, and 7.5% of people did not answer the census question.

Of those at least 15 years old, 8,565 (14.8%) people had a bachelor's or higher degree, 32,001 (55.2%) had a post-high school certificate or diploma, and 14,844 (25.6%) people exclusively held high school qualifications. The median income was $39,000, compared with $41,500 nationally. 4,803 people (8.3%) earned over $100,000 compared to 12.1% nationally. The employment status of those at least 15 was that 29,181 (50.3%) people were employed full-time, 7,746 (13.4%) were part-time, and 2,526 (4.4%) were unemployed.[[16]](#cite_note-Census_2023-16)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/2023_NZ_Census_Population_Density_-_Rotorua_Lakes_District.png/250px-2023_NZ_Census_Population_Density_-_Rotorua_Lakes_District.png)](/wiki/File:2023_NZ_Census_Population_Density_-_Rotorua_Lakes_District.png)

Population density in the 2023 census

## Local government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_District&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Local government")]

### Local council

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_District&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Local council")]

Every three years, a mayor and the district councillors are elected in [local elections](/wiki/Local_elections_in_New_Zealand "Local elections in New Zealand"). In the [most recent elections in 2022](/wiki/2022_New_Zealand_local_elections "2022 New Zealand local elections"), [Tania Tapsell](/wiki/Tania_Tapsell "Tania Tapsell") was elected as mayor and ten councillors were returned. Rotorua Lakes uses the [first-past-the-post](/wiki/First-past-the-post "First-past-the-post") (FPP) voting system and elects six councillors [at-large](/wiki/At-large "At-large"), three for the [Māori ward](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_ward "Māori ward"), and one for the rural ward.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

#### Coat of arms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_District&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Coat of arms")]

Coat of arms of Rotorua Lakes Council

|  |
| --- |
| Notes  The council has a [coat of arms](/wiki/Achievement_(heraldry) "Achievement (heraldry)"). The [blazon](/wiki/Blazon "Blazon") is:[[21]](#cite_note-RotoruaCoA-21)  Crest  On a wreath of the colours a male [huia bird](/wiki/Huia "Huia") standing in a brake of [New Zealand fern](/wiki/Dicksonia_squarrosa "Dicksonia squarrosa") proper.  Escutcheon  [Azure](/wiki/Azure_(heraldry) "Azure (heraldry)") on a [chevron](/wiki/Chevron_(insignia) "Chevron (insignia)") [or](/wiki/Or_(heraldry) "Or (heraldry)") a [rainbow trout](/wiki/Rainbow_trout "Rainbow trout") leaping [proper](/wiki/Tincture_(heraldry)#Proper "Tincture (heraldry)") between in [chief](/wiki/Chief_(heraldry) "Chief (heraldry)") a pine tree and a sprig of [kōwhai](/wiki/K%C5%8Dwhai "Kōwhai") leaved slipped and flowered and a [geyser](/wiki/Geyser "Geyser") issuing from rock in base all also proper.  Supporters  On the [dexter](/wiki/Dexter_and_sinister "Dexter and sinister") side a figure representing a farm settler of the nineteenth century, at his feet a [cattle dog](/wiki/Australian_Cattle_Dog "Australian Cattle Dog") [sejant](/wiki/Attitude_(heraldry)#Sejant "Attitude (heraldry)") and on the [sinister](/wiki/Dexter_and_sinister "Dexter and sinister") side a figure representing a [Māori](/wiki/M%C4%81ori_people "Māori people") chieftain all proper.  Motto  *Tātau tātau* (We together *or* We are one). |

### Sister cities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Rotorua_Lakes_District&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Sister cities")]

Rotorua has four [sister cities](/wiki/Sister_city "Sister city"):[[22]](#cite_note-22)

* [Klamath Falls, Oregon](/wiki/Klamath_Falls,_Oregon "Klamath Falls, Oregon")
* [Beppu](/wiki/Beppu "Beppu"), [Kyushu](/wiki/Kyushu "Kyushu")
* [Lake Macquarie](/wiki/City_of_Lake_Macquarie "City of Lake Macquarie"), [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales")
* The [Wuzhong District](/wiki/Wuzhong_District "Wuzhong District") of [Suzhou](/wiki/Suzhou,_Jiangsu "Suzhou, Jiangsu"), China
